quality.text:
Opus 4.7 at the 1M context window with high or extra-high effort delivers the best code by a clear margin, topping both model and model+effort rankings on deliverable and test quality. For shipping workflows, pair it with PowerShell — powershell/opus47-1m is the #1 deliverable combination by both judges, while typescript-bun/opus47-1m and powershell-tool/opus47-1m lead on test quality.

- **Top performer**: opus47-1m finishes #1 on every model axis (both judges agree, deliverable ρ = +0.90 with only one sonnet/sonnet46 reversal; tests ρ = +0.70).
- **Effort tier**: opus47-1m moving from medium to high/xhigh lifts mean deliverable scores by roughly a full point on one judge and 0.20 on the other; both confirm the step-up, and high and xhigh trade places by less than a tenth, so high is the cost-effective stop.
- **Best by language**: powershell + opus47-1m is the top deliverable pairing by both judges (language×model ρ = +0.75); typescript-bun + opus47-1m leads on tests, with both judges placing it in their top four.
- **Weakest configuration**: haiku45 ranks last on both axes for both judges — baseline agent quality sets a hard floor that no language choice rescues.
- **Where rankings diverge**: language-only ordering has almost no agreement (Tests ρ = +0.00, Deliv ρ = +0.10), so pick the model first and treat language as a secondary decision.

speed_cost.text:
The best speed+cost balance is opus47-200k-medium on the default language: $1.17/run in 4.7 minutes with quality 3.8/3.6. If budget matters more than quality, sonnet46-200k-medium on powershell runs at $0.71/run in 5.3 minutes. At the top end, opus47-1m-xhigh is slower AND pricier than opus47-1m-high in every **language**, and only buys better quality on typescript-bun — skip it otherwise.

- **Cheapest competent config**: sonnet46-200k-medium on powershell, $0.71/run, 5.3 min (quality 3.1/3.2). Haiku45 is cheaper at $0.45–$0.49 but quality craters to 2.0–2.6 — not worth the ~$0.25 saved.
- **Fastest configs**: opus47-200k-medium on bash and default both finish in 4.7 min at $1.09–$1.17/run, with competent quality (3.2–3.8 / 3.6–3.9).
- **Best quality-per-dollar at the high end**: opus47-1m-high on default, $1.95/run, 7.1 min, quality 4.2/4.2.
- **Never pick (dominated)**: opus47-1m-xhigh on default, powershell, and powershell-tool — slower, costlier, and equal-or-worse quality than opus47-1m-high on the same **language**. sonnet46-1m-medium on bash (11.9 min, $1.53) is beaten on every dimension by opus47-200k-medium on bash (4.7 min, $1.09, better deliverable quality).
- **Effort sensitivity**: stepping opus47-1m from medium to xhigh roughly doubles cost and time (bash xhigh peaks at 16.5 min / $2.87/run) with little quality gain over high.

judge_consistency_summary.text:
**🟢 The panel is doing its job:** Both judges agree strongly on which model+effort configurations produce better code (Spearman +0.70 on Tests Quality, +0.90 on Workflow Craft), and no own-model reversal tilts in Haiku's favor — in fact Haiku grades its own family harder than Gemini does (e.g., 15-test-results-aggregator / default / haiku45: Haiku 1 vs Gemini 4 on Tests). The +1.6–2.0 absolute gap between judges is calibration, not bias.

- 👀 **Where to look closer:** the widest disagreements (a judge scoring 1 vs 5, a 4-point gap on a 1–5 scale) cluster on Workflow Craft for 15-test-results-aggregator / default / opus47-1m-medium and 16-environment-matrix-generator / powershell / opus47-1m-xhigh — Haiku sits at the floor while Gemini sits at the ceiling, so a human read of those triples would tell us which judge to trust there.
- 🤓 **Surprise finding:** language rankings are completely uncorrelated (ρ = 0.00 on both axes) — the panel agrees on which model wins but not on which language flatters its output.
- ℹ️ **Recommended next step:** manually adjudicate four or five of the 1-vs-5 rows on tasks 15 and 16 to confirm whether Haiku is under-scoring or Gemini is ceiling-bound.

quality_analysis.text:
Opus 4.7 at 1M context, run at high or extra-high effort, is the strongest configuration on both axes — both judges place it first on Workflow Craft (ρ = +0.90) and first on Tests Quality (ρ = +0.70), with no reversals near the top of either ranking. PowerShell paired with Opus 4.7-1m leads the Workflow Craft language×model board, while PowerShell-Tool and TypeScript-Bun paired with Opus 4.7-1m share the Tests Quality lead.

- **Top performer**: opus47-1m at xhigh and high effort sits at 3.31/4.74 and 3.24/4.94 on Workflow Craft, ahead of every other model+effort cell, and both judges agree on the top three tiers with no reversals among them.
- **Effort tier**: moving Opus 4.7-1m from medium to high lifts Workflow Craft from 2.26/4.74 to 3.24/4.94 — both judges register the gain, and xhigh shows diminishing returns above high.
- **Best by language**: powershell/opus47-1m ranks #1 by Gemini and #2 by Haiku on Workflow Craft, with default/opus47-1m and powershell-tool/opus47-1m close behind (ρ = +0.79 across all language×model cells).
- **Workflow Craft ceiling**: Gemini caps the field at 4.94 for opus47-1m-high, and the +0.90 Spearman across model+effort cells means that ceiling reflects panel agreement, not a single judge's scale.
- **Where rankings diverge**: language ordering scores ρ = +0.00 on both axes — the panel agrees on which model produces better code but not on which scripting language flatters it; haiku45 still lands last across both judges with no reversal.

conclusions.text:
The four decisions below — model, effort tier, context length, and scripting language — use quality numbers that average two LLM-as-judge overall scores for Tests Quality and Workflow Craft.

- **Model choice:** haiku45 runs $0.45–$0.49/run at panel-mean 2.0–2.6 on both axes — a full point below sonnet46-1m-medium ($0.97–$1.53/run) and ~1.5 points below opus47-1m (3.2–4.5); both judges rank opus47-1m #1 (ρ = +0.90 Workflow).
- **Effort (opus47 only):** Medium → high on opus47-1m adds $0.5–$1.1/run for +0.3–0.8 Tests Quality and +0.1–0.8 Workflow Craft. High → xhigh adds another $0.3–$1.2/run, with Tests flat or lower on 4 of 5 languages (typescript-bun the exception, 4.1→4.5).
- **Context length (opus47, sonnet46):** 1m vs 200k at medium effort is a wash — opus47-1m wins powershell Tests (3.9 vs 3.7) but loses bash Workflow (3.2 vs 3.9); sonnet46 shows similar language-by-language splits with no consistent gain.
- **Scripting language:** Specifying language over default moves Tests Quality by up to 0.5 points; typescript-bun leads opus47-1m-xhigh Tests (4.5), powershell leads opus47-1m-medium (3.9), default ties powershell on opus47-1m-high (4.2); judges disagree on standalone language ordering (ρ = +0.00 Tests).

No single configuration leads on cost, duration, Tests Quality, and Workflow Craft at once — weigh these magnitudes against your own priorities.
